lede: A look at August 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Summer McIntosh and Evan Gattis, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Pervez Musharraf
events.desc: The President of Pakistan, Pervez Musharraf, resigns under threat of impeachment.

events.title: Hurricane Alicia
events.desc: Hurricane Alicia hits the Texas coast, killing 21 people and causing over US$1 billion in damage (1983 dollars).

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: Operation Starlite begins: United States Marines destroy a Viet Cong stronghold on the Van Tuong peninsula in the first major American ground battle of the war.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Hardest Day air battle, part of the Battle of Britain, takes place. At that point, it is the largest aerial engagement in history with heavy losses sustained on both sides.

events.title: Blackwater Fire of 1937
events.desc: A lightning strike starts the Blackwater Fire of 1937 in Shoshone National Forest, killing 15 firefighters within three days and prompting the United States Forest Service to develop their smokejumper program.

events.title: Nineteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution
events.desc: The Nineteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution is ratified, guaranteeing women's suffrage.

events.title: 1891 Martinique hurricane
events.desc: A major hurricane strikes Martinique, leaving 700 dead.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Globe Tavern: Union forces try to cut a vital Confederate supply-line into Petersburg, Virginia, by attacking the Wilmington and Weldon Railroad.

events.title: Pendle witches
events.desc: The trials of the Pendle witches and Samlesbury witches, one of England's most famous witch trials, begin at the Lancaster Assizes.

events.title: John White (colonist and artist)
events.desc: John White, the governor of the Roanoke Colony, returns from a supply trip to England and finds his settlement deserted.

births.name: Rosalynn Carter
births.line: 41st First Lady of the United States (died 2023)

deaths.text: Ruth Johnson Colvin, American author and educator, founded ProLiteracy Worldwide
Jim Jeffords, American captain, lawyer, and politician
Honoré de Balzac, French novelist and playwright
Antonio Ferramolino, Italian architect and military engineer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Helena of Constantinople (Roman Catholic Church)
